---
title: "New_omus_vis"
author: "Maddy Enda"
format: html
editor: visual
---

### Overview:

-   In this script, we will be creating a geospatial visualization for a report on the distribution of `Omus sympatry`.

### Import libraries

```{r}
# Import relevant libraries
librarian::shelf(tidyverse,
                 dplyr,
                 stars,
                 sf,
                 readxl,
                 scatterpie,
                 ggtext,
                 patchwork,
                 ggspatial,
                 cowplot,
                 calecopal)
```

### Import the data

```{r}
# Load in the excel file
Omus_forms <- read_excel("C:/Users/Madison.Enda/Desktop/omus_data/Omus_forms.xlsx")
```

### Replace NA with 0

```{r}
# Replace NAs
Omus_forms[is.na(Omus_forms)] <- 0

# Create a species percentage column
Omus_forms <- Omus_forms %>%
  mutate(
    lec_pct = lecontei / Total,
    cal_pct = californicus / Total,
    cyl_pct = subcylindricus / Total,
    met_pct = submetallicus / Total
  )
```

```{r}
### Add points
# Create points from your coordinate dataframe
Omus_forms <- Omus_forms %>%
  separate(
    Coordinates,
    into = c("lat", "lon"),
    sep = ","
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    lat = as.numeric(trimws(lat)),
    lon = as.numeric(trimws(lon))
  )

# Convert the lat and lon columns to points
pts <- st_as_sf(
  Omus_forms,
  coords = c("lon", "lat"),
  crs = 4326
)
```

### Read in the california shapefile

```{r}
# Read in shapefile
shp <- st_read("C:/Users/Madison.Enda/Desktop/omus_data/CA_Counties.shp")

# Convert points to be same
pts <- st_transform(pts, st_crs(shp))
```

### Create bounding box

```{r}
bb <- st_bbox(pts)

bb_poly <- st_as_sfc(bb, crs = st_crs(pts))

bb_poly_proj <- st_transform(bb_poly, st_crs(shp))

shp_clip <- st_crop(shp, st_bbox(bb_poly_proj))
```

```{r}
# =========================
# Load libraries
# =========================
librarian::shelf(
  tidyverse,
  dplyr,
  sf,
  readxl,
  ggplot2,
  scatterpie,
  cowplot
)

# =========================
# Import data
# =========================
Omus_forms <- read_excel("C:/Users/Madison.Enda/Desktop/omus_data/Omus_forms.xlsx")
Omus_forms[is.na(Omus_forms)] <- 0

# =========================
# Parse coordinates
# =========================
Omus_forms <- Omus_forms %>%
  separate(Coordinates, into = c("lat", "lon"), sep = ",") %>%
  mutate(
    lat = as.numeric(trimws(lat)),
    lon = as.numeric(trimws(lon))
  )

pts <- st_as_sf(Omus_forms, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)

# =========================
# Load counties
# =========================
shp <- st_read("C:/Users/Madison.Enda/Desktop/omus_data/CA_Counties.shp")

# Transform EVERYTHING to same CRS (IMPORTANT FIX)
shp <- st_transform(shp, 4326)
pts <- st_transform(pts, 4326)

# =========================
# Clip / central CA
# =========================
shp_clip <- st_transform(shp_clip, 4326)

# Assign points to clipped counties
pts_ca <- st_intersection(pts, shp_clip)
pts_ca <- pts_ca %>%
  mutate(COUNTY_NAME = NAME)  # adjust if needed

# =========================
# Aggregate by county (COUNTS for scatterpie)
# =========================
county_dat <- pts_ca %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  group_by(COUNTY_NAME) %>%
  summarise(
    Total = n(),
    lecontei = sum(lecontei),
    californicus = sum(californicus),
    subcylindricus = sum(subcylindricus),
    submetallicus = sum(submetallicus),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# =========================
# Centroids in SAME CRS
# =========================
centroids <- shp_clip %>%
  st_point_on_surface()

centroids$x <- st_coordinates(centroids)[,1]
centroids$y <- st_coordinates(centroids)[,2]

centroids$COUNTY_NAME <- centroids$NAME

# =========================
# Join data
# =========================
pie_sf <- centroids %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  left_join(county_dat, by = "COUNTY_NAME") %>%
  filter(!is.na(Total), Total > 0)

# Ensure numeric (CRITICAL for scatterpie)
pie_sf <- pie_sf %>%
  mutate(across(
    c(lecontei, californicus, subcylindricus, submetallicus, Total),
    as.numeric
  ))

# Add colors
species_colors <- c("#66CC66", "lightblue", "#E6D2B5", "#F7BB38")

names(species_colors) <- c(
  "lecontei",
  "subcylindricus",
  "submetallicus",
  "californicus"
)

# outline counties with data
shp_clip$has_pie <- shp_clip$NAME %in% pie_sf$COUNTY_NAME
```



```{r}
# =========================
# MAIN MAP (pies)
# =========================
main_map <- ggplot() +

  # counties WITHOUT data (light outline)
geom_sf(
  data = shp_clip %>% dplyr::filter(!has_pie),
  color = "grey85",
  linewidth = 0.4,
  fill = "grey95"
) +

# counties WITH pies (emphasized outline)
geom_sf(
  data = shp_clip %>% dplyr::filter(has_pie),
  color = "grey70",
  linewidth = 1.2,
  fill = "grey95"
) +

  geom_scatterpie(
    data = pie_sf,
    aes(x = x, y = y, r = 0.1),
    cols = c("lecontei", "californicus", "subcylindricus", "submetallicus"),
    color = "grey70",
    linewidth = 0.3,   # add this for clarity
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
  data = pie_sf,
  aes(x = x, y = y, label = COUNTY_NAME),
  size = 8,
  fontface = "bold",
  color = "black",
  nudge_y = 0.15,
  check_overlap = TRUE
) +

  coord_sf(crs = st_crs(shp_clip)) +

  scale_fill_manual(
    values = species_colors,
    name = "Omus species"
  ) +

  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = NULL) +
  theme(
    legend.position = c(1.6, 0.25),     # pushes slightly outside plot
    legend.title = element_text(size = 37, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 28),
    legend.key.size = unit(2, "cm"),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(size= 20, angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size= 20))
    
# =========================
# INSET MAP
# =========================
inset_map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = shp, fill = "grey90", color = "white") +
  geom_sf(data = shp_clip, fill = NA, color = "grey70", linewidth = 1) +
  theme_void()

# =========================
# COMBINE
# =========================
final_map <- ggdraw() +
  draw_plot(main_map, x = -0.05, y = 0, width = 0.75, height = 1) +
  draw_plot(inset_map, x = 0.53, y = 0.23, width = 0.4, height = 0.95)
```



### Export map

```{r}
ggsave(
  filename = "central_california_omus_map.png",
  plot = final_map,
  width = 12,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)
```
